cards.title: Two Sum — Return All Pairs
cards.desc: Given an array and a target, return all pairs that add up to the target. (Pattern Technologies)
cards.approach: Store each element in a HashSet. For every element x, check if (target − x) already exists. Collect unique pairs to avoid duplicates.

cards.title: Pythagorean Triples from Array
cards.desc: Find all (a, b, c) in the array such that a² + b² = c². (Coupa Software)
cards.approach: Square all elements and store in a HashSet. Then use two nested loops for pairs — check if their squared sum exists in the set. Avoids the O(n³) triple loop.

cards.title: Move Zeroes to End
cards.desc: Move all zeros to end while preserving order of non-zero elements. (NetSPI / GeeksforGeeks list)
cards.approach: Use a write-pointer that only advances on non-zero elements. Fill the rest with zeros. Single pass, in-place.

cards.title: Merge Intervals
cards.desc: Given a list of intervals, merge all overlapping ones. (NetSPI)
cards.approach: Sort intervals by start time. Iterate and extend or push. The bottleneck is sorting; the merge itself is O(n).

cards.title: Maximize Meetings in a Room
cards.desc: Given start and end times, select the maximum number of non-overlapping meetings. (NetSPI)
cards.approach: Classic Activity Selection (Greedy). Sort by finish time. Always pick the meeting that ends earliest — maximizes room available for next meetings.

cards.title: Anagram Check
cards.desc: Return true if string t is an anagram of string s. (Pattern Technologies)
cards.approach: Use a 26-size int array (one pass increment for s, decrement for t). If all zeros at the end → anagram. Faster than sort-based O(n log n).

cards.title: Caesar Cipher / Ciphertext Decoder
cards.desc: Decode a shifted string using the known original word to determine the shift. (Pattern Technologies / NetSPI)
cards.approach: Derive shift from the first character of the known word vs its ciphertext equivalent. Then shift all characters back using modular arithmetic (handles wrap-around).

cards.title: Remove Consecutive Special Characters
cards.desc: If 2+ consecutive chars from {a,b,e,t,i,o} appear, remove them. (NetSPI Online Test)
cards.approach: Scan with a start pointer for each run. If the run length ≥ 2, skip. Otherwise append. Single pass over the string.

cards.title: Capitalize Consecutive Target Characters
cards.desc: If 2+ consecutive chars from {a,b,e,i,o,u,s,t} appear, capitalize them. (NetSPI DSA Round)
cards.approach: Two-pass: mark all runs of length ≥ 2, then build output. Or single-pass with lookahead.

cards.title: Count Character Occurrences
cards.desc: Count each character in a string. e.g., "apoorva" → a:2, p:1, o:2, r:1, v:1. (Vervotech)

cards.title: Count '*' Outside Pipe Sections
cards.desc: Count all '*' NOT enclosed between two '|' chars. (Cerillion)
cards.approach: Toggle a boolean "inside pipe" flag on each '|'. Count '*' only when outside.

cards.title: Phone Keypad Letter Combinations
cards.desc: Given a 2-digit string input, return all letter combinations (e.g., "23" → ad,ae,af,bd…). (Vervotech)

cards.title: Binary String Encoder (A/T/C/G)
cards.desc: Encode even-length binary string: 00→A, 01→T, 10→C, 11→G. (Thinkbridge Set C)

cards.title: Stack with getMinValue()
cards.desc: Design a stack supporting push, pop, peek and getMinValue() all in O(1). (Pattern Technologies)
cards.approach: Maintain an auxiliary min-stack in parallel. Push to minStack only when the new value ≤ current min. Pop from minStack only when the main value matches current min.

cards.title: Valid Parentheses Checker
cards.desc: Determine if a string has valid matching pairs of (), [], {}. (Cerillion / Thinkbridge)

cards.title: Segregate Cars by Type (HashMap Grouping)
cards.desc: Input: List<Company<List<Car>>>. Output: Map<type, List<Car>> across all companies. (Pattern Technologies Tech II)

cards.title: Binary Tree Mirror (Invert)
cards.desc: Mirror / invert a binary tree. (NetSPI Technical Interview)

cards.title: Maximum Depth / Height of Tree
cards.desc: Find the maximum depth of a binary tree. (NetSPI / GeeksforGeeks list)

cards.title: Serialize & Deserialize Binary Tree
cards.desc: Encode a binary tree to a string and decode it back. (Thinkbridge Hard)
cards.approach: Use BFS (level-order). Serialize null children as "#". On deserialization, reconstruct level by level using a queue.

cards.title: Delete a Node from Linked List
cards.desc: Delete a node given only access to that node (not the head). (Pattern Technologies)
cards.approach: Copy the next node's value into the current node, then skip the next node. Classic trick — no need for head reference.

cards.title: Flatten a Multilevel Doubly Linked List
cards.desc: Flatten a DLL where nodes may have child sublists. (Veritas)
cards.approach: When a child is found, save the next pointer on a stack. Wire child as next. When list ends, pop from stack to continue.

cards.title: Angle Between Clock Hands
cards.desc: Find the shortest angle between hour and minute hands in 24-hour format (no fixed formula). (Pattern Technologies)
cards.approach: Convert 24h → 12h. Hour hand moves 0.5°/min (30°/hr + 0.5°/min). Minute hand moves 6°/min. Shortest angle = min(diff, 360−diff).

cards.title: Hourglass Star Pattern
cards.desc: Print an hourglass shape taking length as input. (C3IT)

cards.title: Spiral Matrix
cards.desc: Generate an n×n matrix filled with elements 1 to n² in spiral order. (Veritas)

cards.title: Prime Numbers in a Range
cards.desc: Print all prime numbers from a given range. (Vervotech Interview)

cards.title: 2nd Highest Marks
cards.desc: Find the second highest marks in a student table. (Securely)
cards.sqlNote: 💡 Use DENSE_RANK() for ties-aware ranking — avoids subquery nesting.

cards.title: Toggle 0 ↔ 1 in a Column
cards.desc: Update a column to flip all 1s to 0s and 0s to 1s. (Vervotech)
cards.code:
-- Method 1: Using ABS (elegant)
UPDATE your_table
SET   flag = ABS(flag - 1);   -- 1→0, 0→1

-- Method 2: Using CASE (most readable)
UPDATE your_table
SET   flag = CASE WHEN flag = 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END;

-- Method 3: Bitwise XOR (MySQL-specific)
UPDATE your_table SET flag = flag ^ 1;

cards.title: Top 5 Users with Most Article Comments
cards.desc: JOIN users + articles + comments, GROUP and rank. (Pattern Technologies)
cards.code:
-- Schema: users(id, name), articles(id, user_id, title), comments(id, article_id, body)

SELECT
    u.name                    AS user_name,
    COUNT(c.id)               AS total_comments
FROM users u
JOIN articles a ON a.user_id    = u.id
JOIN comments c ON c.article_id = a.id
GROUP BY u.id, u.name
ORDER BY total_comments DESC
LIMIT 5;

-- Extended: with article breakdown
SELECT
    u.name       AS user_name,
    a.title      AS article_name,
    COUNT(c.id)  AS comments_count
FROM users u
JOIN articles a ON a.user_id    = u.id
JOIN comments c ON c.article_id = a.id
GROUP BY u.name, a.title
ORDER BY comments_count DESC;

cards.title: Students NOT Assigned to Any Department
cards.desc: LEFT JOIN + NULL check to find unmatched records. (Securely)

cards.title: Product Name & Total Quantity Sold
cards.desc: JOIN products + orders, GROUP BY product. (Pattern Technologies Round 1)
cards.code:
-- products(id, name), orders(id, product_id, quantity)

SELECT
    p.name             AS product_name,
    COALESCE(SUM(o.quantity), 0) AS total_quantity
FROM      products p
LEFT JOIN orders   o ON o.product_id = p.id
GROUP BY  p.id, p.name
ORDER BY  total_quantity DESC;

cards.title: Sum of All Elements in an Array
cards.desc: JavaScript function to return sum of all array elements. (Cerillion)

cards.title: Chef with 3 Bags (Check-in & Carry Logic)
cards.desc: 3 bags (A,B,C kg). Check in exactly 2, carry 1. Total check-in ≤ D kg, carry ≤ E kg. Can all 3 be taken? (Thinkbridge Medium)
cards.approach: There are only 3 possible combinations (which bag to carry). Check all 3 — if any is valid, return true.

cards.title: Mathematical Expression Evaluator
cards.desc: User inputs an expression with integers, +, -, *, /, and parentheses. Evaluate with correct operator precedence.
cards.approach: Two-stack approach: one for numbers, one for operators. When we see an operator, compare its precedence with the top of the op-stack — if the top has ≥ precedence, pop and evaluate first (handles * / before + -). Parentheses push a sentinel and flush on ')'. No eval() used.

cards.title: String Pattern Replace & Dollar Collapse
cards.desc: First 2 chars form the pattern → replace every occurrence with $. Then collapse any consecutive run of 2+ $ into a single $.
cards.approach:
Pass 1 — slide a 2-char window; when it matches the pattern emit '$', else emit the single char and advance by 1.
    Pass 2 — collapse runs of 2+ consecutive '$' into a single '$' using a toggle pointer.
    Example: "ababcab" → pattern="ab" → "$" + "$c$" → "$c$" after collapse.

cards.title: Caps and Bottles Matching
cards.desc: Comma-separated input of caps (C<n>) and bottles (B<n>). A cap fits its matching bottle only if within 3 positions. Count valid matched bottles.
cards.approach: Parse input into a position-indexed array. Build two maps: capPositions{num→idx} and bottlePositions{num→idx}. For each bottle, check if a cap with the same number exists AND |capIdx - bottleIdx| ≤ 3. Bottles without a matching cap (or cap too far) are not counted.

cards.title: Calculate Total Marks from Assignment Objects
cards.desc: Match student submissions to assignment questions by index. Skip TEXT. Award marks only if MCQ answer matches exactly.
cards.approach: Questions and submissions are parallel arrays (same index = same question). Iterate by index. Skip if type is TEXT. If MCQ and submittedAns === correctAns, add marks. No external libraries — only array/object access.

cards.title: Closest Element to Target
cards.desc: Find the element in the array closest to the given target. No built-in functions. Array can have +ve and -ve numbers.
cards.approach: Manual absolute value: diff = (x ≥ target) ? x - target : target - x. Track the smallest diff seen so far and update closest. If two elements are equidistant, the first one found wins (or handle tie-breaking as needed).
